varieties.name: Super Dwarf Sunset Nectarine™
varieties.traits: Compact dwarf, 1-1.5m height, dark red foliage year-round, high-density pink blossoms, naturally dwarfing
varieties.yield_notes: Good for dwarf size, November-December harvest in subtropics
varieties.availability: PlantNet partner nurseries throughout Queensland, humid subtropical climates garden centers

varieties.name: SunnyBelle™ Nectarine
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety, excellent eating quality, red skin with yellow flesh
varieties.availability: PlantNet partner nurseries, Queensland and WA specialty fruit nurseries

varieties.name: Nectarina™ Nectarine
varieties.traits: Dwarf to medium size, suitable for subtropical coastal areas
varieties.availability: Fruitopia Nursery humid subtropical climates, PlantNet stockists Queensland

varieties.name: Delight-Ful™ Nectarine
varieties.traits: Semi-clingstone, well-shaped large fruit, attractive color

varieties.name: TangO's® Flat Nectarine
varieties.traits: Unique flat/donut shape, small to medium clingstone, distinctive appearance
varieties.availability: Queensland and Western Australia PlantNet nurseries

varieties.name: Sundowner Nectarine
varieties.origin: Australian selection, widely grown in subtropical regions
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, round fruit, lower chill requirement than most
varieties.yield_notes: Good production even in warm winter areas

varieties.name: Sunset Nectarine
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit, adapted for subtropical areas
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable in warm climates, October-November harvest warmer areas

varieties.name: Royal Gem™ Nectarine
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, premium quality, deep red skin on creamy background

varieties.name: Goldmine Nectarine
varieties.origin: New Zealand introduction, early 1900s, widely grown heritage variety
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, round fruit, red-speckled over yellow skin
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable cropper, heavy crops, February ripening

varieties.name: Snow Queen Nectarine
varieties.traits: Very large, fair-skinned with light russet blush, taste test winner
varieties.yield_notes: Very large fruit, abundant crops, late June-early July California (later in humid subtropical climates)

varieties.name: Crimson Baby Nectarine
varieties.traits: Relatively compact tree, medium-sized fruit, smooth skin

varieties.name: Fantasia Nectarine
varieties.traits: Large fruit, bright yellow-red skin, very adaptable
varieties.yield_notes: Vigorous, productive, late July-early August (later in humid subtropical climates)

varieties.name: Flavortop Nectarine
varieties.traits: Large fruit, smooth deep red skin over pale to golden yellow

varieties.name: Arctic Rose White Nectarine
varieties.traits: New generation white-flesh, picked firm they are taste sensation
varieties.yield_notes: Mid to late July California, very high scoring in taste tests
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, may require sourcing from interstate

varieties.name: Early Rivers Nectarine
varieties.origin: Heritage variety, 1800s-early 1900s, widely grown historic cultivar
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit, old variety, tender and juicy, heritage status
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable bearer, early fruiting, early January Australia

varieties.name: Necta Zee (Nectazee) Dwarf
varieties.traits: Genetic dwarf, compact tree for small gardens, container-suitable

varieties.name: Tuscany™ Dwarf Nectarine
varieties.traits: Dwarf on dwarfing rootstock, prolific bearer, medium to large fruit
